message:
db+cli: stop flipping verdict to Broken on benign WAL-too-small; bump self-update body limit

Closes #99.

Primary fix (db/pool.rs): remove "wal file too small" from
is_corruption_error_message. A truncated/header-only WAL is a
*recoverable* sidecar state, not data corruption. The main DB image
still passes PRAGMA integrity_check = ok. Classifying the string as
corruption was causing the mailbox verdict engine to escalate to
Broken/Corrupt on every startup after a disk-full incident, silently
disabling the MCP read surface (write surface + CLI route were not
honoring the same gate, hence the split-brain behavior in the report).
The string stays classified as a recovery-error via
is_sqlite_recovery_error_message, so the WAL sidecar still gets
cleaned up — it just doesn't escalate to Broken.

Init-time cleanup (db/pool.rs): extend cleanup_empty_wal_sidecar to
remove WAL sidecars of size <=32 bytes (header-only), not just <32.
A header-only WAL contains no frame data (minimum valid frame is 24-byte
header + page_size bytes, and min page size is 512), so removal is
safe — SQLite will recreate it on the next write. This short-circuits
the "WAL file too small for header during rebuild: read 0, need 32"
error at the source rather than just tolerating it downstream.

Self-update side-fix (cli/lib.rs): raise download_file_sync's body
limit from asupersync's default 16 MB to 256 MB via HttpClient::builder().max_body_size().
Release tarballs already ship both am and mcp-agent-mail binaries at
~33 MB, so `am self-update` was unreachable — users had to re-run
install.sh to upgrade. 256 MB leaves room for future binary growth
without going unbounded.

Tests:
- corruption_error_does_not_flag_wal_too_small (new): the WAL string
  must NOT match is_corruption_error_message.
- recovery_error_detects_wal_file_too_small (revised): the WAL string
  still matches is_sqlite_recovery_error_message.
- cleanup_empty_wal_sidecar_removes_header_only_wal (new): 32-byte WAL
  is removed.
- Existing zero-byte and >32-byte WAL tests still pass unchanged.

Not addressed in this commit (user explicitly called these out as
expected-behavior items):
- Split-brain gating: writes and the CLI route ignore the allows_writes
  flag that the MCP read surface honors. Worth aligning but needs
  design discussion on which direction to align (gate everything vs.
  surface both in the verdict response).
- MCP read-error detail surfacing: DbError::Sqlite/Schema map to the
  generic "A database error occurred" string. The detail is in the
  structured payload but not in the user-visible message. Worth
  improving but out of scope for this specific crash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
